statusDefinitions.label: Validated (Win)
statusDefinitions.description: Primary metrics met; safe to scale or ship.

statusDefinitions.label: Insightful (Partial Learning)
statusDefinitions.description: Partial learning: not a full win, but insight worth acting on — iterate and retest.

statusDefinitions.label: Halted (Negative / Guardrails)
statusDefinitions.description: Stopped: material harm or no path to value without redesign.

experiments.name: Bid non-linearly on install probability to concentrate spend on high-value opportunities
experiments.summary: Replace linear, rule-heavy bidding with a simpler nonlinear formula driven by install probability to win more on high P(install) requests and less on long-tail.
experiments.businessGoal: Increase win rate on auctions where a user is very likely to install, and less where they are not, without wrecking ROAS or margin.
experiments.description: The DS test replaced a linear, heavily rule-based bid (using an older `prediction_installs` gating) with a simpler, nonlinear formula driven by install probability, to push spend toward users/requests the model believes are more likely to install.
experiments.hypothesis: Variant B amplifies bids for high install-probability users and dampens them for low-probability ones, more aggressively than a straight linear multiplier, producing more volume where the model is confident and a different cost and revenue profile.

control.name: Variant A (Control, ~90% of traffic)
bullets: A **linear bid** from cost-per-install and install probability, plus extra rules (tiers) from batch metric `prediction_installs` and segment logic.

test.name: Variant B (Test, ~10% of traffic)
bullets: **Non-linear (power-law) bid** based on install probability only, simpler segment rules (e.g. Engages Installers with Engaged users; no high/medium/low `prediction_installs` steps as in Control).

kpis.name: Avg. win rate (normalized)
kpis.context: Test won more auctions; win rate for top requests (install_proba over 5%) +97% vs low (under 1%) down about 5%.

kpis.name: Traffic-normalized installs
kpis.context: More conversions from Test traffic, not just volume.

kpis.name: D1 ROAS
kpis.context: Revenue per $ spend, first-day window — looked better in Test on charted period.

kpis.name: eCPI (efficiency of buying installs)
kpis.context: Expected: bidding up to win “better” users may raise CPI.

kpis.name: Margin (profit / activity)
kpis.context: Gating for the business. Margin is not cohorted in the source doc; between-group relationship is as stated.

bullets: Variant B won more auctions, increased traffic-normalized installs, and showed higher D1 and D3 ROAS.
eCPI increased materially; margin was worse in Test than Control even with better short-window ROAS.
Test won on engagement and short-window ROAS; did not meet margin. Needs fine-tuning before wider exposure — valuable learning.
resultsNarrative.tableSummary: Reach / win rate up: normalized impressions and win rate higher, especially for high P(install). Funnel: installs and IR up (normalized). Revenue: D1/D3 ROAS and D1 RR up. eCPI up. Margin: significantly more negative in the Test cell vs Variant A.

learnings: Reaching high probability install users improved conversion, indicating strong discriminatory power in ML models
Next: cap bids per campaign/segment (`max_acceptable_bid`) and re-run. Longer term: bid shading.
